description: US-425 signature-help output eval — golden cases over the bundled gst-3.2.5 cartridge (no gst, no VS Code). Drives the real `signatureHelpAt` provider with the SignatureCandidate set assembled exactly as server.ts does (workspace method selectors ∪ kernel keyword selectors). The `▮` marks the cursor; per case we assert the active parameter (the keyword being filled), the keyword-prefix union (selectors that must / must not appear), the active signature (the fully-typed selector), and the null cases (non-keyword cursors / unknown selectors). Works offline (AC2).

cases.name: AC1 first keyword: active parameter 0, the at:-prefix union
AC1 second keyword: active parameter 1, prefix narrows to at:put:
AC1 do: prefix surfaces do: and do:separatedBy:, do: is active
AC1 inject:into: tracks the second keyword
AC1 nested keyword argument is skipped (do: inside parens is not the outer message)
AC1 a workspace keyword method surfaces in its own union
cases.text:
Object subclass: Foo [
  greet: x [ ^self ]
  run [ ^self greet: ▮ ]
]

cases.name: AC1 unary-send cursor → no signature help
AC1 receiver/head cursor → no signature help
AC1 binary-send cursor → no signature help
AC1 unknown keyword selector → no signature help
